name: Chig Okonkwo
headline: Steady move tight end heads to Washington seeking a target bump
body:
Okonkwo arrives in Washington as a known commodity who profiles as a stable, low-ceiling TE2. Across the last three seasons in Tennessee he hovered in the same lane: 70 to 79 targets, a 14 to 16 percent target share, and finishes between TE17 and TE22. His 2025 line tells the story plainly, 56 catches on 79 looks for 560 yards and just 2 scores, with a 70.9 percent catch rate and only 68 fantasy points. The efficiency was respectable, a 0.16 EPA per play and 1.3 yards per route run, but the volume never spiked.

The red-zone usage is the real anchor on his outlook, with five red-zone targets and three inside the 20 producing minimal touchdown equity. He plays a lot, a 63.7 percent snap share on a 69.3 percent route rate, yet generates yardage on short, secure throws averaging 4.63 air yards per target. A new offense could nudge his role, but until the target count and scoring climb, he stays a streaming-tier option.
